title: NpcProposal
description: Schema for an NPC Agent's proposed reaction. NPCs NEVER write to the world state directly — they emit a Proposal that the Resolver validates and (possibly) merges. This enforces the write-domain-isolation rule from the 《崇祯》reference: only the Resolver touches canonical state.

proposalId.description: Unique proposal identifier. Resolver uses (runId, proposalId) for idempotency.

runId.description: Run identifier; must match the run the agent is currently bound to.

characterId.description: NPC character ID that is reacting. Must be present in the world snapshot's beliefMatrices.

triggerPlayerActionId.description: The clientActionId that triggered this proposal, if any. null for ambient / director-driven proposals.

proposedAction.description: Atomic action the NPC wants to take. Same 12-value vocabulary as PlayerAction to keep action space uniform.

targetId.description: Character ID this proposal targets (often the player). null for self / environment.

speechIntent.description: The pragmatic intent behind the NPC's words. The Resolver maps this to a dialogue template + belief-update signature.

referencedMemoryIds.description: Memory IDs the NPC is drawing on for this proposal. Resolver MUST verify each ID is in the current recall set; ungrounded memories are rejected (anti-hallucination).

subject.description: Character or concept the belief is about (e.g. 'player', 'father', '1937_photograph').

newState.description: Target belief state for the subject. Resolver clamps transitions to the legal set.

confidence.description: How confident the NPC is in this belief shift. Clamped to [0,1].

evidenceMemoryId.description: Memory that grounds the belief shift; null if purely emotional.

beliefUpdatesRequested.description: Belief deltas the NPC wants applied to either itself or a target.

to.description: NPC's proposed emotion after the turn. Must differ from 'from' to be non-trivial.

intensity.description: Strength of the emotion. Resolver clamps to [0,1] and may dampen by character stability trait.

emotionalTransition.description: Affective transition; one of the four key components that drive believable NPC reactions.

reasonCodes.description: Typed reason codes that explain WHY the NPC proposed this action. The Resolver cross-checks reason codes against recent events to detect LLM fabrications.

confidence.description: Agent's overall self-assessed confidence in the proposal. Low-confidence proposals are passed through but flagged in the resolver audit log.

expectedContradictions.description: Belief IDs or character IDs whose current state would contradict this proposal. Honest agents populate this; dishonest ones won't, and that's a Resolver signal.

allOf.description: If speechIntent is 'reveal_truth' or 'conceal_truth', the proposal MUST reference at least one memory to ground the claim.
emotionalTransition.to MUST differ from .from (no-op emotion updates are forbidden).
